Time & Clock Bilingual Classroom Labels

EN-ES Printable Resource

Time & Clock Bilingual Classroom Labels

A set of bilingual (English-Spanish) labels, routine cards, and rules posters designed for K-2 classrooms to introduce and reinforce time-related vocabulary and daily routines.

TopicTime & Clock
Age BandK-2
Pack Typeclassroom labels
LanguageEN-ES

✨ What's Inside

  • 12 classroom labels
  • 5 routine cards
  • 4 classroom rules
  • Teacher notes & answer key

📖 How to Use

Place full-size labels near the classroom clock, calendar, or a dedicated 'Time' learning center. Use small labels on objects or areas to indicate 'now' (e.g., current activity bin) or 'later' (e.g., future activity bin). Display visual routine cards in a prominent location, reviewing them daily. Post classroom rules where they are easily visible and refer to them frequently.

Classroom Rules

2

Raise your hand.

3

Be kind to friends.

Sé amable con tus amigos.

4

Clean up your space.

Limpia tu espacio.

Mar 18, 2026lanternell.comPage 5 / 6
LanternELLTeacher Notes📄 Printable Pack

Teacher Notes

🎯 Objective

To introduce and reinforce basic time-related vocabulary and daily classroom routines in both English and Spanish, supporting language acquisition and classroom management for K-2 ELL newcomers.

💡 Suggested Use

Place full-size labels near the classroom clock, calendar, or a dedicated 'Time' learning center. Use small labels on objects or areas to indicate 'now' (e.g., current activity bin) or 'later' (e.g., future activity bin). Display visual routine cards in a prominent location, reviewing them daily. Post classroom rules where they are easily visible and refer to them frequently.

📦 Materials Needed

  • Printer
  • Laminator (recommended for durability)
  • Scissors
  • Tape or adhesive for mounting
  • Velcro dots (optional, for interactive routine charts)
  • A classroom clock with clear numbers (digital and analog if possible)

🔀 Differentiation Tips

  • Pair visuals with gestures and actions for each word/routine.
  • Encourage students to repeat the words in both languages.
  • Use sentence frames (e.g., 'It is [time].', 'First we do [routine], then we do [routine].')
  • Integrate the labels into daily conversations and calendar time.
  • For rules, role-play scenarios to demonstrate expected behaviors.
📄 Terms of Use — personal-classroom-use
